statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a 2D array maze, where 0 represents an empty cell and 1 represents a wall.
statement.outputFormat: Output is a boolean indicating whether it's possible to place walls in the maze to create a unique solution that allows only one way to enter and exit.
statement.notes: The maze is a square with an odd number of rows and columns. You can place walls at any empty cell. The start and end points are marked as (0, 0) and (rows-1, cols-1) respectively.

constraints.description: Number of rows in the maze
Number of columns in the maze

examples.explanation: No valid placement of walls to create a unique solution.

hints.text: Try to find a recursive relation between the number of walls in the sub-maze and the number of possible paths.

editorial.title: Maze Solver with Wall Placement Editorial
editorial.intuition: Use a backtracking approach to try all possible placements of walls in the maze.
editorial.bruteForce: Start by placing walls at all empty cells in the maze and then check if only one path exists.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a backtracking approach to try all possible placements of walls and use the number of walls in the sub-maze and the number of possible paths to prune the search space.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the number of walls in the sub-maze and the number of possible paths to prune the search space and find a unique solution.
commonMistakes: Inadequate pruning of the search space
